perturbation.original_text: 5.6. Inspection of API. Within thirty (30) calendar days of the arrival of each lot of API at the manufacturing facility designated by INTERSECT, INTERSECT shall inspect and test each lot of API at its own cost and expense. If, upon inspecting and testing the API, INTERSECT determines that a lot of API does not conform to the Product Specifications, then INTERSECT shall, within such thirty (30) day period, give HOVIONE written notice of such non-conformity (setting forth the details of such non-conformity):Unless HOVIONE objects, within 20 working days from the notice by INTERSECT, to the non-conformity INTERSECT will return the non-conforming API to HOVIONE.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.6. Inspection of API. Within fifteen (15) calendar days of the arrival of each lot of API at the manufacturing facility designated by INTERSECT, INTERSECT shall inspect and test each lot of API at its own cost and expense. If, upon inspecting and testing the API, INTERSECT determines that a lot of API does not conform to the Product Specifications, then INTERSECT shall, within such thirty (30) day period, give HOVIONE written notice of such non-conformity (setting forth the details of such non-conformity):Unless HOVIONE objects, within 20 working days from the notice by INTERSECT, to the non-conformity INTERSECT will return the non-conforming API to HOVIONE.
perturbation.explanation: The modification reduces the time for inspecting API from 30 to 15 days, while the original text maintains that INTERSECT has 30 days to notify HOVIONE of non-conformity. This creates an in-text contradiction because INTERSECT now has less time to inspect the API but the same amount of time to report issues, potentially making it impossible to complete both tasks within the specified timeframe. This contradiction occurs in the same section, 5.6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.6. Inspection of API. Within fifteen (15) calendar days of the arrival of each lot of API at the manufacturing facility designated by INTERSECT, INTERSECT shall inspect and test each lot of API at its own cost and expense. If, upon inspecting and testing the API, INTERSECT determines that a lot of API does not conform to the Product Specifications, then INTERSECT shall, within such thirty (30) day period, give HOVIONE written notice of such non-conformity (setting forth the details of such non-conformity):Unless HOVIONE objects, within 20 working days from the notice by INTERSECT, to the non-conformity INTERSECT will return the non-conforming API to HOVIONE.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the inspection period from 30 to 15 days. However, the contradicted text, which is the same section, retains the 30-day timeframe for INTERSECT to provide written notice of non-conformity to HOVIONE. This creates a direct conflict and inconsistency within the same section (5.6) because INTERSECT now has a shorter period to inspect the API but is still held to the original 30-day period for reporting non-conformities. This represents a clear in-text contradiction and impacts enforceability by creating ambiguity in timeline obligations, as described by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 10.1. Term. Unless terminated in accordance with the provisions of Section 10.2 below, the term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue in effect for a FIVE (5) year period.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.1. Term. Unless terminated in accordance with the provisions of Section 10.2 below, the term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue in effect for a TEN (10) year period, unless INTERSECT provides written notice of non-renewal after FIVE (5) years.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by initially stating the agreement is for a TEN (10) year period, but then allowing INTERSECT to terminate it after FIVE (5) years with written notice. This contradicts the initial term length of ten years and introduces uncertainty regarding the actual duration of the agreement. This contradiction is mainly contained within Section 10.1 but can affect all the sections related to Term and Termination, since the first phrase explicitly mentions a term of ten years, which is immediatly followed by the posibility of non-renewal at 5 years.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.1. Term. Unless terminated in accordance with the provisions of Section 10.2 below, the term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue in effect for a TEN (10) year period, unless INTERSECT provides written notice of non-renewal after FIVE (5) years.
perturbation.justification: The changed text initially states a TEN (10) year term but allows INTERSECT to terminate after FIVE (5) years with written notice. This creates a contradiction within the same section, as the agreement's duration becomes uncertain. A ten-year agreement, followed by an allowance to terminate after five years, is a direct contradiction that affects other sections related to Term and Termination, aligning with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition. This directly breaks compatibility.
